title: Workbook — Justice
html:
Student Workbook

For use after class or in groups. Print this page or work in your notebook.

Recalling the essentials

  1. What is the classic definition of justice that the Catechism incorporates? Why does it matter that it is a «constant and firm will»?
  2. Name and explain the three types of justice. Give a concrete example of each from your daily life.
  3. What does the «universal destination of goods» mean? Why is private property not an absolute right?
  4. Name the four fundamental principles of the Church's social doctrine and briefly explain each one.
  5. What does the Catechism say about restitution? Why doesn't forgiveness cancel it?

For reflection and writing

Answer each question sincerely. There are no «correct» answers to these personal questions.

1. What things do you owe other people that you have not yet paid back or returned?

Make a list of 3 concrete things. Not just money: time, attention, recognition, apologies, gratitude. Be specific with names and situations.

___________ ___________ ___________

2. If Jesus told you today, as he told Zacchaeus: «Come down, for I am staying at your house today,» what would change in your economic life? What expense would embarrass you? What debt would you repair?

Be brutally honest. The answer is between you and God.

3. Of the sins against the seventh commandment (theft, fraud, usury, unjust wages, corruption, tax evasion, harm to creation), which one seems most normalized in your culture? Have you participated in that normalization?

For example: «everyone cheats on taxes,» «you can't get ahead without bribes,» «it's just business.» Think about those phrases people say without thinking.

4. Imagine you are the owner of a company with 200 employees. What would your wage policy be? How much would you earn compared to the lowest-paid employee? Why?

The gap between the highest and lowest salary at a company says a lot about its idea of justice.

5. Have you forgiven a grave injustice without giving up on justice being done? How do you do that? What does Our Lady of Guadalupe teach us about how God restores justice without vengeance?

Because justice without mercy is vengeance. But mercy without justice is complicity.

Practical activities

A. Zacchaeus's balance sheet

Make your own «justice balance sheet» Zacchaeus-style. In one column, write what you have received without deserving it (gifts, opportunities, privileges). In another, write what you have taken without right or not paid. In a third, write what you commit to giving back, to whom, and by when. Don't file it away: act this week.

What I have received in excess What I owe or have taken I commit to giving back Deadline
    
    
    

B. A concrete work of mercy

Choose one of the fourteen works of mercy (seven corporal, seven spiritual) and do it this week. No excuses: it must be concrete, with a concrete person. Then write here what happened:

 

 

 

 

 

C. Letters to Romero and Guadalupe

Write two mini-letters (6-8 lines each): one to St. Óscar Romero, asking him something about justice that troubles you. Another to Our Lady of Guadalupe, asking her to teach you to see the poor as she sees them.

To St. Óscar Romero:

 

 

 

 

To Our Lady of Guadalupe:

 

 

 

 

Verse to memorize

«The effect of righteousness will be peace, and the result of righteousness, quietness and trust forever.»

— Isaiah 32:17

Prayer for this week

Lord Jesus, you who entered Zacchaeus's house and awakened justice in him,
enter my life and show me what I need to give back.
Give me the courage to repair the harm I have caused,
the honesty to pay fairly those who work for me,
and an open heart to share with those who have the least.
Help me to understand that true peace
is only born from the justice your Spirit sows.
Amen.

Print Workbook
